questions.question: How many components does the Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI) evaluate?

options.text: Four
Five
Seven
Ten

questions.explanation: The PSQI evaluates seven distinct components of sleep quality: subjective sleep quality, sleep latency, sleep duration, sleep efficiency, sleep disturbances, use of sleep medication, and daytime dysfunction.

questions.question: What total PSQI score generally suggests poor sleep quality that could benefit from improvement?

options.text: Above 3
Above 5
Above 10
Above 15

questions.explanation: A total PSQI score above 5 suggests that your sleep quality could benefit from improvement. Scores of 0-5 indicate good sleep quality, 6-10 indicate poor sleep quality, and 11-21 indicate concerning sleep quality.

questions.question: What does 'sleep efficiency' measure in the PSQI?

options.text: How quickly you fall asleep
How many hours of deep sleep you get
The percentage of time in bed that is actually spent sleeping
How rested you feel upon waking

questions.explanation: Sleep efficiency measures the percentage of time you spend in bed that is actually spent sleeping. For example, if you are in bed for 8 hours but only sleep for 6, your sleep efficiency is 75%.

questions.question: What does 'sleep latency' refer to?

options.text: How long you stay asleep during the night
How long it takes you to fall asleep
How often you wake up during the night
How much daytime sleepiness you experience

questions.explanation: Sleep latency refers to the amount of time it takes you to fall asleep each night. A sleep latency of 15 minutes or less is considered ideal.

questions.question: Why is the PSQI described as an educational starting point rather than a clinical diagnosis?

options.text: It is too short to be accurate
It was developed for research, not clinical use
Only a healthcare professional can diagnose sleep disorders through comprehensive evaluation
The scoring system is unreliable

questions.explanation: The simplified educational version of the PSQI helps you reflect on your sleep patterns and build self-awareness, but only a healthcare professional can provide a clinical diagnosis through comprehensive evaluation.

title: Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index - Self-Assessment - Quiz
